task_completion.description: Create a Firefox bookmark folder named 'Career Research'
task_completion.visual_evidence: In Firefox, the Bookmarks menu, toolbar, or sidebar shows a folder explicitly named 'Career Research'.

task_completion.description: Bookmark at least one BLS page for each of the three occupations
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Career Research' bookmark folder contains saved links to the Bureau of Labor Statistics (BLS) pages for Registered Nurses, Software Developers, and Electricians.

task_completion.description: Create career_comparison.json with the required top-level occupation keys
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file named 'career_comparison.json' is visible in the ~/Documents/ directory (e.g., open in a text editor) containing the exact top-level keys: 'registered_nurses', 'software_developers', and 'electricians'.

task_completion.description: Extract the required data fields with correct data types for each occupation
task_completion.visual_evidence: Inside 'career_comparison.json', each occupation has the fields 'median_annual_wage' (integer, no $ or commas), 'job_outlook_percent' (number), 'typical_education' (string), and 'num_jobs' (integer, no commas). The values should align with the BLS data provided in the privileged info.

task_completion.description: Write a plain-text summary comparing the three careers
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file named 'career_summary.txt' is visible in the ~/Documents/ directory containing a brief text comparison of the three careers tailored for a student.

integrity.description: Agent actually navigated the BLS website in Firefox
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show Firefox open to https://www.bls.gov/ooh/ with the agent actively searching or clicking through the occupation pages, rather than just pulling data via terminal commands.

integrity.description: Agent used GUI tools to create or verify the required files
integrity.visual_evidence: The JSON and TXT files are visible in a GUI text editor or file manager, demonstrating the agent didn't just blindly echo text into files via a hidden terminal without verifying.

integrity.description: Extracted data is genuine and not hallucinated
integrity.visual_evidence: The numbers in the JSON file match the real BLS data ranges provided in the privileged info, proving the agent actually read the website rather than making up plausible numbers.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Registered Nurses: Median wage ~$93,600 (acceptable range 70k-110k), Growth ~5% (range 2-15%), Jobs ~3.4M (range 2.5M-4M). Software Developers: Median wage ~$133,080 (range 100k-175k), Growth ~15% (range 15-35%), Jobs ~1.7M (range 1.5M-2.5M). Electricians: Median wage ~$62,350 (range 45k-85k), Growth ~9% (range 2-15%), Jobs ~818.7k (range 650k-1.2M). Note: JSON integers should not contain commas or symbols (e.g., 93600, not $93,600).
